root_cause: HMDB (hmdb.ca) is protected by Cloudflare bot challenge pages. All programmatic requests — including .json/.xml metabolite endpoints, the /simple/api route, /downloads bulk files, and search — return HTTP 403 or a Cloudflare JS challenge page. The site requires JavaScript execution and cookie validation, which is not feasible in a server-side REST client. Access requires either a browser session or contacting the HMDB team (eponine@ualberta.ca) for an API key.
retry_notes: Check if HMDB has released a public API key program or relaxed Cloudflare rules. Contact samackay@ualberta.ca for institutional API access.
workaround: HMDB_get_metabolite and HMDB_search use PubChem as a cross-reference (HMDB IDs are stored as RegistryID in PubChem). HMDB_get_diseases uses the CTD (Comparative Toxicogenomics Database) API to retrieve chemical-disease associations given the metabolite name resolved from PubChem.
investigation_notes: curl https://www.hmdb.ca/simple/api → Cloudflare JS challenge (200 HTML with cf-chl-opt)
CTD batchQuery works: GET /tools/batchQuery.go?inputType=chem&inputTerms=glucose → HTTP 200 JSON

attempted_config.name: HMDB_get_metabolite
attempted_config.description: Get metabolite data directly from HMDB API by HMDB ID

attempted_config.name: HMDB_search
attempted_config.description: Search HMDB metabolites by name or formula

attempted_config.name: HMDB_get_diseases
attempted_config.description: Get disease associations for a metabolite from HMDB
attempted_config.failure: HTTP 403 Cloudflare; also no documented open API endpoint for disease data
